Latest Headlines
TinkerList joins SVG Europe as a Bronze sponsor
By Jo Ruddock | Wednesday, December 7, 2022 - 09:29
sponsorTinkerList
Belgium’s TinkerList has joined SVG Europe as a Bronze sponsor. With creative teams often still losing too much time focusing on the technological infrastructure of broadcasts instead of creating content, as well as facing shrinking budgets while the volume and speed of video content creation is rising, TinkerList has developed a customisable platform and automation […]  More
Live from Qatar: Capturing content on mobiles for a ‘digital first’ World Cup
By George Bevir | Tuesday, December 6, 2022 - 17:22
FIFA World Cup QatarHBSQatar 2022
For the first time ever at a World Cup, content for rights holders is being captured using mobile phones. It’s part of a strategy that represents the biggest commitment to a ‘digital first’ approach of any football tournament or possibly even sport generally, says Tim Stott, executive producer, HBS Digital. “What we’re saying to broadcasters […]  More
5G and AI top the agenda at Vislink
By Jo Ruddock | Tuesday, December 6, 2022 - 10:42
SportTech Journalvislink
“We have made continuous enhancements to our 5G and AI-powered solutions over the past year in terms of performance, compatibility and efficiency of operation,” says Michel Bais, chief product officer, Vislink. “Notably, we have been featured participants in several advanced 5G trials for live sporting events.” Vislink participated with BT Media & Broadcast and the […]  More
Qatar 2022: Kevin Callahan of Fox Sports on the IP transition and return of match-day control rooms
By Ken Kerschbaumer, SVG Editorial Director | Tuesday, December 6, 2022 - 09:27
footballUSworkflows
The 2022 FIFA World Cup has shifted from the grind of the Group Stage, with four matches a day, to the knockout stage. For the Fox Sports production team, getting to this point has taken years of planning and months of onsite activity to ensure that new workflows and old workflows mesh and help deliver […]  More
France Summit 2022: Watch video highlights from the Paris event
By Will Strauss, Editor | Tuesday, December 6, 2022 - 08:43
franceolympicsrugbytennis
On 24 November 2022, the French sports broadcasting and production community came together for its annual evening of panel discussions, presentations, and networking. Topics included the French Open tennis, Paris 2024 Summer Olympics and the Rugby Union World Cup 2023. You can now view edited highlights from the event which are in French with English […]  More
Qatar 2022: FIFA’s Florin Mitu on the evolution of World Cup coverage
By Ken Kerschbaumer, SVG Editorial Director | Monday, December 5, 2022 - 15:24
footballProductionTechnology
Florin Mitu is the head of broadcast production for the 2022 FIFA World Cup in Qatar and this year’s effort has been years in the making. And while the COVID pandemic and the global supply chain crisis impacted efforts, innovations like a move to IP, new workflows in UHD and HDR, and additional broadcast enhancements […]  More
Access All Areas: How (and why) Sky Sports took viewers ‘behind the scenes’ on the EFL
By Will Strauss, Editor | Monday, December 5, 2022 - 15:20
footballProductionuk
Sunday’s English Football League (EFL) League 1 match between Wycombe Wanderers and Portsmouth saw two teams with promotion ambitions go head to head in front of the live TV cameras on Sky Sports. The home team won the game 2-0 but the match was perhaps as noteworthy for the way it was covered by the […]  More
Tech Insight: MediaPro’s Emili Planas Quintana on meeting the demand for more content, without sacrificing quality levels
By John Maxwell Hobbs | Monday, December 5, 2022 - 10:26
footballspainTechnology
“Nobody in this business is working if they don’t love the job,” says Emili Planas Quintana, CTO at MediaPro. “What I love about my job is integrating creativity with technology within a budget.” Quintana studied cinema at university. “In theory, I am closer to a cinema director than a CTO,” he says. His time as […]  More
Qatar 2022: Timeline TV on providing groundbreaking joint facilities for ITV Sport and BBC Sport
By Heather McLean, Editor | Monday, December 5, 2022 - 08:49
bbc sportITV SportTimeline TVWorld Cup 2022
“This is one of our biggest and most complex projects to date. Timeline are providing end to end facilities for both the BBC and ITV Sport, as a joint operation for both broadcasters,” says Quinn Cowper, Timeline TV’s head of engineering. Timeline TV is trailblazing with the provision of joint facilities and services for both […]  More
Live from Qatar: On-site in Doha with Eurovision Sport’s Stefan-Eric Wildemann
By George Bevir | Sunday, December 4, 2022 - 07:43
2014 FIFA World CupebuEurovision Sport
The EBU’s Eurovision Sport division is in Doha for the World Cup, as part of its remit to ensure that major sporting events are available free-to-air across Europe. While FIFA has direct deals with broadcasters in the UK, Germany, France, Italy and Spain and the Nordics, the EBU has a collective agreement with FIFA for […]  More
